title: helianthemum syriacum subsp. thibaudii
intro: Helianthemum syriacum subsp. thibaudii, also known as the Thibaud's rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Cistaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant and cytotoxic effects. The herbal preparations of Helianthemum syriacum subsp. thibaudii include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions, including skin disorders and respiratory issues.
